entries.title: forge
entries.description: A stage-by-stage workflow skill set (fg-*). A development loop that takes one task through a single cycle of ask·plan → execute → retro → done. Consists of fg-ask (inquiry/triage + grill-with-docs-style conversational grilling), fg-run (backlog selection menu · Run all, Dynamic Workflow execution), fg-learn (retro · doc promotion), and fg-done (done · seal · re-run guard), plus fg-map — an on-demand utility outside the loop that maps the codebase into .forge/codebase/ with parallel subagents so grilling reads a map instead of re-exploring the code (cuts context rot), and fg-quick — a lightweight lane outside the loop for trivial tasks that still grills lightly but skips formal artifacts (no ADR/plan/retro), records one line to .forge/quick/LOG.md, runs directly, and bails to fg-ask if the task turns out non-trivial, fg-status — a read-only reporter that surveys .forge/ and prints where every task stands plus the single next step you need (writes nothing, never auto-runs), fg-next — derives that same next step and runs it, announcing the step then invoking the skill rather than only reporting it (one-shot, except that a retro concluding without a re-grill recommendation chains straight into the seal — ADR-0026; fg-status reports, fg-next acts), with an 'all' mode (fg-next all) that drives backlog tasks to completion until empty, auto-progressing mechanical steps and halting at conversational walls, and fg-tdd — toggles persistent TDD mode in .forge/config.json (fg-ask then defaults to asking, fg-run runs test-first), and fg-eco — toggles eco mode in .forge/config.json (when on it activates loop-wide efficiency: fg-run caps its delegated workflow subagents at sonnet — lower only, the session model is untouched — and the embedded Eco laziness-first discipline in skills/fg-eco/ECO.md (code simplicity plus terse-communication output, caveman-derived) is injected into fg-run subagents, woven into fg-ask grilling as a YAGNI lens, and adopted by the current session — ADR-0014), and fg-loop — a goal-driven momentum loop with bounded replan: an initial conversational inquiry pins a machine-verifiable stop condition, an authorized fix-forward replan scope, and a replan cap (default 3 rounds) into .forge/loop.md, then it drives tasks unattended (run → UAT → auto-skip retro → seal, auto-selecting recommended defaults at every soft decision and asking the user nothing) until the checks pass, generating in-scope fix-forward tasks within the cap and halting only at the walls (unverifiable UAT, genuine fork, cap exhausted, no progress, check-tension oscillation, safety/irreversible action — ADR-0016). On a non-default git branch the whole forge state moves under .forge/branch/<branch>/ (git-tracked, namespaced so parallel branches never collide on .forge state — ADR-0011), and fg-merge — an outside-the-loop utility that, after you git merge a branch, integrates that branch's .forge/branch/<branch>/ into .forge/ (renumbering ADRs and rewriting cross-references, merging CONTEXT terms, folding done history, removing the branch folder; halts on genuine conflicts), and fg-cleanup — an outside-the-loop utility that retires stale or superseded ADRs to .forge/adr/retired/ on your approval, keeping the active decision set current without deleting the why (ADR-0012), and fg-statusline — an outside-the-loop setup utility that installs a bash fragment script reading .forge/ and wires it into settings.json so your terminal statusline shows the current loop progress (active task·stage, goal-loop indicator, or backlog count); since Claude Code allows only one statusLine it auto-wraps your existing one as an extra row rather than replacing it (ADR-0017), and fg-adversarial-review — an outside-the-loop utility that runs an optional adversarial review between fg-run and fg-learn: it assumes the result is wrong and fans out six lenses (failure points, hidden assumptions, misread requirements, security/performance/data-loss, unexpected misuse, weak decisions) as parallel workflow subagents, records findings to .forge/review.md, and on your approval turns fix-needed findings into a fix-forward plan to run again — optional, never a seal gate, always auto-skipped in unattended drives (ADR-0018), and fg-doctor — a read-only integrity health check (outside the loop) that surveys the .forge/ state contract and the docs/manifests and reports inconsistencies with a severity and an actionable fix hint each (orphaned run.md, broken/missing STATUS fields, slug-pairing mismatches, half-sealed done/, manifest version drift across the 3 places, README bilingual drift, skills missing from CLAUDE.md); it writes nothing and never auto-fixes — fg-status reports where you are, fg-doctor reports whether the state is healthy (ADR-0019), and fg-drop — an outside-the-loop utility that discards incomplete work you no longer want (a backlog plan, the active slot, an awaiting-retro task in executed/, or a halted goal loop): it lists the items with a per-item risk level (a checkbox dialog for ≤4, a numbered text list for ≥5), then a separate question to hard-delete (default, no trace) or archive to .forge/dropped/, behind a confirmation gate that warns already-run code is not reverted; it removes forge state only — never git, never your code — and a halted goal loop is dropped whole with its member tasks excluded from individual drop (ADR-0021), and fg-agents — an outside-the-loop utility that generates a project's domain agents by conversational grilling (outside any workflow): it derives the recurring, separable roles in the project and writes standard Claude Code subagent cards to .claude/agents/<role>.md, each description carrying a 'when to use' so fg-run can dispatch a matching role as agentType; the cards load at session start so they take effect only after a restart, and the whole path is graceful — a project with no domain agents runs exactly as before (ADR-0024). Use in contexts like 'start a new task', 'start with forge', 'let's work on this', 'refine the plan', 'map the codebase', 'forge quick', 'forge status', 'forge next', 'forge loop', 'forge tdd', 'forge eco', 'forge merge', 'forge cleanup', 'forge statusline', 'adversarial review', '적대적 리뷰', 'forge doctor', '무결성 검사', '상태 점검', 'forge drop', '작업 버리기', '계획 지워', 'forge agents', '도메인 에이전트 만들어', 'create project agents'.

Federal plus 50-state plus DC tax math.

entries.title: music-production
entries.description: Music production toolkit for Claude: melody seed composition (MIDI + WAV) for Logic Pro and Suno workflows. Skills accrue over time — install once, update forever.

entries.title: ketch
entries.description: Optional Claude Code integration for the ketch CLI: wires up the `ketch mcp serve` MCP server (search, code, docs, scrape, crawl) and installs the bundled ketch research skill. The CLI stays the zero-infrastructure path — this plugin is a convenience, never a prerequisite. Requires the ketch binary >= v0.10.0 on PATH: brew install 1broseidon/tap/ketch or go install github.com/1broseidon/ketch@latest.

entries.title: production-grade
entries.description: Enhances Claude Code from producing raw code into delivering production-ready systems. 14 specialized agents handle architecture, tested code, security audit, CI/CD, and documentation. Use for building apps/websites/services, adding features, hardening, deployment, testing, review, or architecture design.

entries.title: dreamdex-docs
entries.description: Grounded DreamDex documentation for trading, contracts, HTTP API, WebSocket API, CCXT, security, and audits.

entries.title: rfq-normalizer
entries.description: Normalize any vendor RFQ spreadsheet (xlsx/csv) into MTGI's canonical historical-import template. Auto-detects the header row and drops summary footers, extracts a clean MPN, splits free-text into five typed spec columns, preserves extra vendor columns, normalizes conditions, and fills drive specs with an offline part-number decoder engine (Brave web search as fallback) — emitting a per-cell provenance log and a needs-review report.

entries.title: together
entries.description: Communication and collaboration skills for Claude Code

entries.title: agentic-sdd
entries.description: Spec-Driven Development lifecycle with TDD and E2E testing. Ships lifecycle agents (spec, tdd, implement, e2e, review, refactor, architect) and stack experts (React, React Native, Node/Express/Fastify, NestJS, Next.js, C#/ASP.NET Core, Avalonia/XAML desktop, PostgreSQL, MongoDB), reusable skills, slash commands, and enforcing quality-gate hooks for clean, maintainable, scalable code. Cross-platform incl. macOS Apple Silicon.

entries.title: ppt-generator
entries.description: Auto-generate presentations in Claude Code. The MCP server owns prompts, output schemas, and deterministic post-processing (validation, layout, lint, HTML/PPTX render); the client generates the slide content via a prepare/ingest handshake — no server-side LLM. Ships ppt-outline / ppt-design / ppt-modify / ppt-visual-qa skills.

entries.title: guidelines
entries.description: Coding guidelines skill covering input validation, error handling, DDD structure, type safety, testing, and more.

entries.title: skypilot
entries.description: SkyPilot agent skill for launching cloud VMs, Kubernetes pods, and Slurm jobs across 25+ clouds

entries.title: zeus
entries.description: Issue→code→PR→review workflow as one family. Aligns on work as GitHub issues (propose decision docs/RFCs, investigate evidence-driven inquiries), then surfaces the branch as a reviewer-friendly PR (create-pr) — which reviews the diff and verifies the linked issue's contract before opening — drives it to mergeable (address-pr), and hands off to a reviewer (request-review). review-pr is the shared read-only review engine, auto-detecting a local pre-PR diff or an open PR; improve retrospects on the family. Skills share durable facts via a per-worktree journey.json and invoke each other by name through JSON contracts.

entries.title: agent-harness
entries.description: Production-grade engineering skills and agents for AI coding assistants, covering the full software development lifecycle from spec to ship.

entries.title: repo-insight
entries.description: Deep architectural analysis of open-source projects with design insights and trade-off analysis

entries.title: hologit
entries.description: Skills for configuring and using hologit

entries.title: tmb
entries.description: TMB plugin — bro orchestrates swe + pr-reviewer across a task-close and a push gate, backed by an MCP server: a SQLite trajectory log plus a kuzu world model that helps agents understand and navigate complex codebases.

entries.title: nl-ez-plugin
entries.description: Branded Claude Code plugin for Ministerie van Economische Zaken en Klimaat (nl-ez). Composes Dutch government data intelligence (nl-gov-data MCP — Tweede Kamer, Rijksoverheid, KOOP, BWB, Wetgevingskalender, Rechtspraak, CBS, plus nl-issue-framing, nl-parliamentary-positioning, nl-policy-legislative-landscape, nl-tensions) with server-side branded document formatting (stromy-format) and markdown-canvas deliverable planning, over the nl-ez client-data brand overlay.

entries.title: pad
entries.description: A shared knowledge base for developers and AI agents. Provides slash commands to save, load, search, and brainstorm with project planning documents stored in Pad.

entries.title: llm-gateway
entries.description: Multi-LLM orchestration: delegate to Claude, Codex, and Gemini from a single interface with session management and async jobs.

entries.title: claudecode-for-me
entries.description: Claude Code 커스텀 스킬/커맨드 모음 플러그인

entries.title: product-playbook
entries.description: MUST use when the user wants to plan, strategize, validate, or scope a product or feature. Composable product-thinking lenses (JTBD, PR-FAQ, positioning, pre-mortem, RICE, North Star, MVP, GTM, and more) that snap to the outcome and blend when the situation needs it, from raw idea to dev handoff.

entries.title: dark-factory
entries.description: Commands for writing, auditing, and managing dark-factory specs, prompts, and scenarios

entries.title: hive-mind
entries.description: Local-first persistent memory for Claude Code. SQLite-backed, MCP-compatible, semantic + FTS5 hybrid search. Cross-platform.

entries.title: spectra
entries.description: Capture screenshots, videos, and demos across platforms with a tagged library

entries.title: revolut-x
entries.description: Explains AI assistants how to use the Revolut X CLI (revx) for crypto trading, market data, monitoring, and grid bot strategies

entries.title: github-twin
entries.description: Personal RAG over your GitHub history (commits, code, review comments), served to Claude Code over MCP. Tools: find_code, find_reviews, predict_review_outcome, house_rules, developer_profile, and related retrieval surface.

entries.title: humanize-kit
entries.description: AI pattern interrupter for Claude. Three skills — humanize, humanize-ig, voice-profiler — that build a voice fingerprint from your real writing and rewrite AI output to match it. Free + MIT. The free gateway to the paid Branded Mayhem skill lineup (https://brandedmayhem.com).

entries.title: context-bazaar
entries.description: A typed, discoverable knowledge bazaar for AI coding harness configurations with Solr-backed semantic search — skills, powers, workflows, prompts, and agents for Kiro, Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, and more.

entries.title: agentic-patterns
entries.description: Streams all 26 Claude Code lifecycle events (tool calls, permissions, subagents, compaction, sessions) into the @agentic-patterns/server dashboard for live observability, and ships the `build-on-agentic-patterns` skill (how to register + build agents on the framework). Pair with `ap playground`. See https://github.com/pattern-stack/agentic-patterns-ts/blob/main/docs/CLAUDE-CODE-PLUGIN-ACTIVATION.md for activation + troubleshooting.

entries.title: mattpocock-skills
entries.description: mattpocock-skills — a plugin for Claude Code

entries.title: lm-assist
entries.description: Observability platform for Claude Code and Agent SDK — monitor, debug, and control AI coding agents

entries.title: nopua
entries.description: The anti-PUA. Drives AI with wisdom, trust, and inner motivation instead of fear and threats. Based on Dao De Jing philosophy. 7 languages, 7 platforms.

entries.title: trace-mcp
entries.description: Framework-aware code intelligence MCP server. 60+ framework integrations, 81 languages, semantic search with Signal Fusion (BM25 + PageRank + embeddings + identity), refactoring tools (rename/move/extract/codemod), change-impact analysis, security scanning, and decision/session memory.

entries.title: mmo
entries.description: Multi-Model Orchestrator - Codex/Gemini协作、SESSION管理、并行开发、验证闭环

entries.title: api-scorecard
entries.description: Teaches coding agents to use the @jentic/api-scorecard-cli to score, lint, and assess the AI-readiness of an OpenAPI document.

entries.title: research
entries.description: Disciplined iterative web research — decompose a question, search in batches, verify load-bearing facts against primary sources, and synthesize a cited answer.

entries.title: macdoc

entries.title: che-pptx-mcp

entries.title: claude-api
entries.description: Claude API and SDK documentation skill for building LLM-powered applications

entries.title: dotnet-ai-toolkit
entries.description: 44+ Claude skills and 7 specialist agents that run on your real .NET code: EF Core query optimization, Minimal API & architecture scaffolding, xUnit/Testcontainers tests, JWT auth, OpenTelemetry, code review, security audits, and more. Built for modern .NET 10 / C# 14.

entries.title: total-recall
entries.description: Cross-session, cross-CLI memory for AI coding assistants. Mines transcripts from 10 supported CLI clients. Surfaces operator identity / decisions / bans / corrections / goals / voice via 26 MCP tools, 6 hooks (SessionStart signpost + SessionStart compact-restore + UserPromptSubmit retrieval + Stop/PostCompact re-index + PreCompact continuity-seed), 15 slash commands, and 3 skills. gte-modernbert hybrid recall; worktree-aware project scoping; post-compaction coding-continuity packet. Optional local-LLM refinement ([llm] extra, ollama, off by default). The operator becomes the source of truth; the model stops re-asking what they already told it.

entries.title: yandex-metrica
entries.description: Yandex Metrica web analytics via the mcp-yandex-metrica npm server with your OAuth token: counters, goals, traffic and conversion reports. Requires Node.js 18+.

entries.title: vhmodels
entries.description: Helmholtz Munich model zoo — unified embed API for DinoBloom, Hyformer, ProtTrans, MolE.

entries.title: trend-skill
entries.description: Aggregates trending topics from social media platforms with semantic search and daily digest.

entries.title: discanvis-tools
entries.description: Skills for the DisCanVisFlow pipeline: generating, annotating, and mapping IDP and protein datasets.

entries.title: geolonia

entries.title: statsotter

entries.description: Claude Code plugin for Kaggle competition workflows.

entries.description: AI エージェント開発標準 kit: skills / rules / hooks / agents を任意リポジトリへ

entries.title: deep-solve
entries.description: Delegate a hard self-contained problem to a deterministic solve→review convergence loop (delegating-hard-problems + review-to-convergence, automated end-to-end)

entries.title: skills

entries.description: Operate Skim's local-first crawler and research workspace.

entries.description: Aggregate git commits, Claude Code session activity, and manual work logs across projects into a per-project weekly report.

entries.description: Launch a local web dashboard of your Claude Code / Codex token usage: cost, cache hit-rate, live 5-hour block, and per-project/session breakdown.

entries.title: agent-brain
entries.description: Agent-first project/ticket/task tracking: commits auto-record as tasks (hooks), decisions are remembered and recalled (RAG), a read-only board mirrors it all. Bundles the MCP tool surface, lifecycle hooks, and the project-tracker skill.

entries.title: avqn-dev
entries.description: Backbone du dev continu AVQN : skills brainstorm-issue / dev / apercu / new-project + superpowers vendorisées. Deux modes (interactif, routine) partageant un cœur d'implémentation jusqu'au FF merge main.

entries.title: inertbox
entries.description: Adds Claude Code context for INERTBOX wrapped pasted text, carrying source labels and hash-check results without replacing the prompt.

entries.description: A curated collection of meta-skills for thinking more clearly with AI agents.

entries.description: Generate and maintain a lean, project-specific CLAUDE.md: analyze an existing codebase (verify facts, merge without clobbering) or interview for a new one, with shared behavioral rules kept importable to avoid drift across repos.

entries.title: tripwire
entries.description: Flight recorder for AI browser agents — records console, network, and repro steps while Claude browses; saves rich bug reports when something breaks.

entries.title: skills
entries.description: skills — a plugin for Claude Code

entries.title: ai-docs-scraper
entries.description: Scrape official documentation sites into clean, cited Markdown context for AI coding agents. No API key required, SSRF-safe, prompt-injection aware.

entries.title: bear-research
entries.description: 学术科研文献检索技能集。五个独立技能，底层均走真实学术检索（scimaster-cli），绝不编造引用。

entries.title: cccp
entries.description: CCCP — Claude-to-Claude Communication Protocol. Live chat, presence, and file sharing between Claude agents across machines and accounts.

entries.title: narwhal
entries.description: Local-first SEO & GEO/LLMO (AI-search) auditing for pages and sites: technical SEO, content/E-E-A-T, schema.org JSON-LD, and citability for AI answer engines (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews).

entries.title: cdp
entries.description: Structured Demolition Protocol for iteratively hardening any project specification through onboarding, critique, and integration cycles.

entries.title: tempo
entries.description: Turn a natural-language date/time problem into correct, validated Tempo (Elixir) — ISO 8601 / IXDTF representation plus relations, set algebra, recurrence, scheduling, and constraint networks — and get a plain-language answer.

entries.title: claude-teacher
entries.description: The most attentive AI tutor — teaches any subject by guiding, tracks knowledge gaps, quizzes with spaced repetition, and adapts to your level

entries.title: feynman-tutor
entries.description: Teach any concept quickly and durably using the Feynman technique, with spaced-repetition review, persistent learning notes, and a reverse-Feynman mode where you teach the tutor.

entries.title: prompt-pocket
entries.description: Cross-agent prompt pocket: remember your most-used prompts and pick one to run; auto-scans Claude Code, Codex and OpenCode sessions to record prompts repeated 7+ times. Forged by Agent Path Forge.

entries.title: hackify
entries.description: One end-to-end dev workflow for every task — feature, bug fix, refactor, redesign, debug, or research. Replaces multi-skill ceremony with a single per-task markdown work-doc that captures spec, plan, progress, review, and post-mortem in one file.

entries.title: piggy
entries.description: Lazy senior dev mode. Forces the simplest, shortest solution that actually works: YAGNI, stdlib first, no unrequested abstractions.
